title: Go Oiwa to become new Japan national team coach from 2025, with Moriyasu until Asian Cup
summary: The Japan Football Association announced the appointment of Oiwa as the next head coach while Moriyasu extends his term by six months.

title: Record temperature of 41.1°C in Hamamatsu, highest of the year in Japan
summary: Several regions recorded extreme values and heatstroke alerts were issued as a heatwave affects the country.

title: 'Secondary capital' bill pits opposition against ruling party in Japan
summary: The initiative to transfer functions to Osaka will be voted on July 24 amid strong opposition criticism.

title: EU imposes record 166 billion yen fine on Google for abuse of dominant position
summary: The penalty, the largest ever imposed by the EU, is in response to the promotion of its own services in search results.

title: ASEAN foreign ministers' meeting concludes with progress in Myanmar and brief Japan-China contact
summary: The forum discussed the situation in Myanmar, the South China Sea, and the Middle East, and allowed a bilateral meeting between Motegi and Wang Yi.

title: Kirishima ties for lead in Nagoya sumo tournament after defeating Onosato
summary: The champion earned his tenth victory and shares the top spot with three other wrestlers with three days remaining.

title: Films by Kore-eda Hirokazu and Tsukamoto Shinya to compete at Venice Film Festival
summary: Two Japanese directors have been selected for the official section of the prestigious film festival.

title: United States and Saudi Arabia sign civil nuclear cooperation agreement with flexible terms
summary: The pact has raised concerns about possible nuclear proliferation in the region, as it excludes other companies.

title: European Central Bank holds interest rates amid geopolitical uncertainty
summary: The institution expects to assess the impact of the previous hike and price developments in the Middle East.

title: Former Osaka city official arrested again over alleged 8 million yen bribery
summary: The former section chief allegedly received money in exchange for favorable contracts.

title: Businessman arrested for evading 360 million yen in taxes using 'Angel Tax' system
summary: The accused allegedly illegally used tax benefits for startup investments.

title: Houthi rebels attack Saudi vessel in Bab el-Mandeb strait
summary: The incident raises tension in the Red Sea shipping route after attacks in the Gulf of Oman.

title: JAXA successfully completes combustion test of Epsilon S rocket engine
summary: The Japanese space agency confirmed the operation of the improved engine after two previous failures.

title: Princess Kiko visits Wajima to oversee group medical checkups after earthquake
summary: The princess visited the earthquake-hit area to encourage victims and expressed concern about the heat.

title: High school student drowns in Kochi's Kagami River
summary: The minor was rescued unconscious from the riverbed and taken to the hospital, where he died.

title: Supreme Court to review acquittal of defendant in Inawashiro Lake boat accident
summary: The highest court will examine whether the boat operator is guilty of the deaths of three people in 2011.

title: Takuma Inoue and Tenshin Nasukawa agree to rematch for WBC bantamweight world title
summary: The fight will take place in September after Inoue won by decision in the first encounter.

title: Prime Minister Takaichi announces 3 billion yen in aid for Solomon Islands
summary: Japan strengthens its presence in the Pacific with a development package in a shift away from Chinese influence.

title: Former Japan Finance Corporation employee arrested for alleged bribery with luxury watches
summary: The accused allegedly received a high-end watch and other favors in exchange for facilitating loans.

title: Liberal Democratic Party urges government to strengthen artificial intelligence personnel and units
summary: The request aims to leverage the latest advances in generative AI to improve the country's competitiveness.

title: Woman injured by stray bullet while driving in Akita
summary: The victim felt a strong impact in her ear and police are investigating if she was hit by a hunting shot.

title: LDP secretary general urges Prime Minister Takaichi to get more sleep after her post on X
summary: The leader had written that she sleeps between 0 and 3 hours, sparking concern among her colleagues.
